semantic_sweep.review_note: Reviewed the complete chapter, all twenty-nine exact source mappings, three proof targets, eleven Lean declarations, phase-record fixture and gate harness, every manifest atom, and every prose candidate. Made the Evidence-Gated Phase Unlock Contract the distinct owner; expanded 18/12/18/18; preserved zero phase completion and zero core effect; and froze the real-program matched-baseline causal reproduction and transfer campaign.

scope.population: Exact programs, roadmap versions, phases, dependencies, artifacts, gates, authorities, evaluators, evidence transitions, debt, residuals, rollback paths, reviewers, consumers, environments, and times.
scope.environment: Twenty-nine mappings; one phase-record schema/fixture; two valid and six invalid dependency-gate cases; one synthetic expired-evidence check; three targets; eleven finite declarations. No real phase completion, deployed controller, natural workload, matched baseline, independent reproduction, or transfer.
scope.model: Evidence-Gated Phase Unlock Contract over phase state, typed dependencies, evidence-first order, authority, evaluation, debt, terminal decisions, correction, rollback, matched baselines, monitoring, and expiry.
scope.authority: Owns phase-unlock sequencing only; intent, implementation, evaluation, evidence transition, capability, safety, readiness, release, rights, publication, and successor-roadmap authorities remain separate.
scope.time: Reviewed through 2026-07-15; material objective, dependency, interface, artifact, gate, evaluator, authority, threat, resource, consumer, environment, or time changes expire affected contracts.
scope.artifact: Chapter, manifest, twenty-nine mappings, phase record, fixtures, validator, three targets, eleven declarations, semantic review, and future campaign.

assumptions: Program phases, dependencies, artifacts, commands, environments, resources, gates, evaluators, debt, residuals, rollback, and decisions can be observed and versioned.
Research, demo, integration, promotion, and release states can be separated in practice.
Strong program-management baselines can receive matched teams, tasks, tools, resources, and time.
Independent teams, reviewers, institutions, infrastructures, legal regimes, environments, and successor programs can reproduce and transfer outcomes.
chapter_defaults.strongest_counterclaim: A typed roadmap is governance theater: competent teams can sequence work with ordinary backlogs and stage gates, while extra contracts add latency, false blocks, stale paperwork, and no unique safety or usefulness benefit.
chapter_defaults.falsifier: Matched natural programs show no reduction in false unlocks, rework, incidents, evidence latency, or recovery; unacceptable throughput and burden; gate theater or debt erasure persists; or results fail independent reproduction and transfer.

required_lanes.necessity: Freeze implementation lineage and external comparators without inheriting results.
Justify objective, affected-party, authority, exception, appeal, debt acceptance, stop, and terminal decisions.
Specify finite phase and dependency properties without claiming real program correctness.
Run real phases, commands, artifacts, failures, incidents, rollback, correction, and propagation.
Measure throughput, time-to-evidence, false unlocks and blocks, rework, incidents, debt, burden, resources, recovery, and total cost.
Ablate phase states, dependency gates, evaluator independence, debt, terminal decisions, and rollback.
Reproduce across teams, projects, models, organizations, infrastructures, legal regimes, environments, and time.

contrary_evidence: The current phase gate harness is synthetic and repository-local.
No phase in the roadmap is accepted, complete, or executed as a controlled natural program.
The eleven declarations reduce hand-authored finite records, routes, and fixture summaries.
The twenty-nine mappings include lineage, variants, source-note-only records, and unreproduced external results.
known_confounds: Team quality, task difficulty, resources, tools, organizational incentives, and management skill can dominate program outcomes.
Outcome-aware graph design, gates, baselines, stopping, attacks, and discarded work can create selection bias.
Shared authors, evaluators, tools, data, organizations, or infrastructure can create false independence.
A complete phase packet can encode false artifacts, weak gates, hidden effects, understated debt, or incorrect terminal decisions.
acceptance_criteria: Bind exact program, phase graph, artifacts, commands, environment, resources, authority, evaluators, debt, residuals, rollback, reviewers, consumers, time, and non-claims.
Run natural multi-phase programs against matched ordinary baselines with complete denominators and adversarial dependency paths.
Demonstrate correction, revocation, expiry, debt, and effect-complete rollback propagation across descendants and consumers.
Promotion survives causal ablation, independent reproduction, and heterogeneous transfer.
narrowing_criteria: Narrow every result to exact graph, phase state, team, task, tool, model, evaluator, organization, infrastructure, legal regime, environment, and time.
Separate proposed, blocked, research, demo, integrate, promote, release, failed, rolled-back, retired, and superseded states.
Unknown gate adequacy, evaluator independence, causal benefit, rollback, cost, reproduction, or transfer remains blocked.
refutation_criteria: Refute unlocks with missing prerequisites, circular dependencies, invalid authority, stale or weak gates, unretired debt, absent evidence transition, or incomplete rollback.
Refute benefit when matched baselines do as well or extra burden, selection, weak attacks, or hidden failures explain outcomes.
Refute phase completion, capability, safety, deployment, transfer, AGI, ASI, or SOTA from roadmaps, fixtures, validators, or finite proofs.
deprecation_criteria: Deprecate only after every phase, dependency, artifact, gate, authority, debt, residual, decision, correction, rollback, consumer, and result migrates with lineage.

reproduction.command: python3 scripts/validate_protocol_examples.py && python3 scripts/validate_prototype_phase_gates.py && cd lean && lake build AsiStackProofs.PrototypeRoadmap
reproduction.environment_lock: Repository-local phase fixtures, validator, and module current through 2026-07-15; real-program campaign not run.
reproduction.evaluator_identity: Repository-local; independent teams, reviewers, institutions, infrastructures, and transfer programs required.

residual.next_unblocking_condition: Run a prospectively frozen natural multi-phase program against matched ordinary baselines with adversarial dependencies, causal ablations, complete outcomes and costs, effect-complete rollback, independent reproduction, and transfer.

non_claims: No roadmap, milestone, dashboard, phase record, fixture, theorem, validator, build, or useful demo establishes phase completion, capability, governance effectiveness, safety, deployment, transfer, AGI, ASI, or SOTA.
No real phase completion, deployed controller, natural matched program, independent reproduction, or transfer exists.
The argument-exit campaign is future work, not a result.

prototype-roadmap.core.strongest_counterclaim: Ordinary competent program management matches the benefits with less burden.
prototype-roadmap.core.falsifier: Natural matched programs show no benefit, unacceptable false blocking or cost, persistent theater, ineffective rollback, or no reproduction and transfer.

prose_review_lineage.retired_sentence: A polished schedule or demo that cannot expose those records has failed the chapter's core contract even if it ships useful work.

preserved_disposition.rationale: Semantically reviewed as bounded explanation, source report, lifecycle restatement, scanner fragment, finite-proof boundary, or explicit non-claim; material obligations are already atomized.

prose_review_lineage.retirement_reason: The reader-facing ownership sentence was clarified without changing its adjudicated meaning; the old sentence hash is no longer current prose.

non_claims: The chapter core remains argument and its support-state effect is none.
No phase has been accepted or completed by this sweep.
Formal declarations enforce finite record boundaries and do not prove program outcomes.
External sources are comparators and do not certify the roadmap.
